assessment_principle: Use metagenomic signals (compositional patterns) NOT pathway presence/absence for vitamin decisions. Pathway presence ≠ vitamin production.

interpretation_cautions.evidence_level: These signals are based on observational studies, systematic reviews, and Mendelian Randomization (MR) analyses. MR uses genetic variants to infer causality — stronger than observational but cannot establish clinical thresholds.
interpretation_cautions.predictive_accuracy: No microbiome-based vitamin deficiency prediction has been validated with standard classification metrics (AUC, sensitivity, specificity). These signals are HYPOTHESIS-GENERATING, not diagnostic.
interpretation_cautions.clinical_standard: Serum/plasma biomarkers remain the gold standard. Microbiome signals SUPPLEMENT, never replace, clinical testing.
interpretation_cautions.population_limitations: Most evidence from European-ancestry or Middle Eastern populations. Transferability uncertain.
interpretation_cautions.directionality: Only vitamins with documented microbial biosynthesis (B12, biotin, folate, B-complex) have plausible predictive logic. For vitamin D, microbiome is likely a CONSEQUENCE of deficiency, not a predictor.

B12_cobalamin.signal_type: INVERSE — higher producer abundance paradoxically associated with deficiency
B12_cobalamin.do_NOT_use: B12 biosynthetic pathway abundance or B12-producing taxa abundance
B12_cobalamin.why: B12 deficiency triggers adaptive/compensatory mechanism — microbiota increases producer abundance, but vitamins may be structurally inactive or poorly absorbed

B_ovatus.meaning: Higher abundance associated with B12 deficiency (inverse signal)

exploratory_MR_signals._evidence: Hou et al., 2025 — Two-sample MR (n=18,340, 24 cohorts, European ancestry). EXPLORATORY: clinical thresholds NOT validated.

primary_FDR_significant.direction: Higher abundance → higher odds of B12 deficiency
primary_FDR_significant.note: Counter-intuitive. Only AmII phylogroup has B12 biosynthesis genes (propionate). AmI (type strain) lacks them (succinate). Genus-level mixes both.

exploratory_MR_signals.implementation_note: These signals supplement, not replace, clinical B12 assessment. Use 'may suggest' language only.

category_1_always_supplement: Vegan/vegetarian: 50-100 mcg daily OR 2000 mcg weekly (indefinite)
Age ≥50: 500 mcg daily OR 2000 mcg weekly (indefinite, 10-30% have atrophic gastritis)
Metformin use: supplement regardless (inhibits ileal absorption)

B9_folate.signal_type: DIVERSITY-DEPENDENT — diversity correlates with RBC folate, but ex vivo production does NOT

Bacteroides.meaning: Primary folate producers (40-65% have the pathway)

Bifidobacterium.meaning: Produce 2x more folate in exponential growth phase

B9_folate.red_flag: Loss of Bacteroides / gain of Prevotella enterotype → reduced folate production potential

B7_biotin.signal_type: LIMITED PRODUCER — only 4 species have documented biosynthesis

producer_count.meaning: Very limited microbial biotin production capacity

B7_biotin.note: ~20% of microbiota completely lacks biotin synthesis genes and COMPETES with host for biotin
B7_biotin.serum_warning: Serum biotin is NOT reliable — can remain falsely normal despite deficiency

B_complex_B1_B2_B5_B6.signal_type: COMPOSITION-DEPENDENT — production depends on microbiota composition, not pathway presence

Bacteroides.meaning: PROTECTIVE for B1, B2, B5, B6 production

red_flags: Crohn's/IBD: reduced Firmicutes = reduced B-vitamin capacity
Western diet: 19-51% lower vitamin biosynthetic gene abundance

K2_menaquinones.signal_type: POOREST — metagenomic data insufficient for K2 assessment
why_poor: Most long-chain MKs in colon where bile-independent absorption uncertain
Production varies by species, growth conditions, community interactions
No established threshold for 'adequate' microbial K2
K2_menaquinones.better_approach: Use dietary fermented food intake + vitamin K1 assessment as primary signals

Akkermansia.meaning: Loss of keystone species (K2 producer among others)
Akkermansia.action: Consider K2 100-180 mcg MK-7 if age >50 + low bone density

step_1.question: Does client meet ANY Category 1 criteria?
step_1.if_yes: Proceed to Category 1 protocol — supplement regardless of microbiota
step_1.if_no: Go to Step 2 — check microbiota metrics

step_2_microbiota_checks.action: Folate 400-600 mcg daily (3-6 months) if dietary intake also low
B12 50-100 mcg daily OR 2000 mcg weekly (serum confirm)

drug_interactions: Folate + anticonvulsants: may need higher doses
B6 + levodopa: may antagonize effect (separate by 2+ hours)
K2 + warfarin: risk of INR reversal (<180 mcg usually safe)
B1 + loop diuretics: increased urinary losses

form_preferences.Folate: Folic acid standard; methylfolate only if MTHFR concerns
